turns.content: What brings you in today?
It's this burning pain right here, after I eat. Upper stomach. Been happening for months. I'm a chef, so eating is part of my day, and it's getting hard to ignore.
When did this start?
Probably around five, six months ago. Noticed it more after the holiday menu changes at work. Just figured it was stress or something.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
Right here, in the middle. Like a hot, gnawing ache. Not sharp, just a constant burn.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No. No fever or anything like that. Just the burning.
What medications are you currently taking?
Just ibuprofen sometimes when it's real bad. Nothing else.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No. Not really. Just the occasional headache. No surgeries or anything either.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, not that I know of. Except penicillin, makes me break out in hives. But I've never needed much else.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
Antacids sometimes, but they don't really help much. A glass of milk can ease it for a bit, but it comes right back.
Does anything make it better or worse?
The burn is worst about 30 minutes after a heavy meal. Greasy or spicy stuff makes it worse, and lying down after eating seems to crank it up. Standing or sitting upright helps a little.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
Makes me irritable during service. Can't taste things properly when I'm in pain, and I'm snapping at my line cooks. Not great for business.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I drink most nights. Part of the job, really - unwinding after service, tastings. And yeah, I smoke about half a pack a day.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
My father had stomach issues. Always reaching for the antacids after dinner. Not sure what it was exactly. He never went to a doctor for it.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No. Never like this. Just occasional heartburn before, but nothing that stuck around for months. This is different.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, maybe a 3 or 4 — it's not killing me, but it's there all the time after I eat. Emotionally... I'd say a 2. Not panicking, just annoyed that I can't seem to fix it myself.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that covers it. Just want to get this checked out so I can focus on work without the pain.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Just powering through. Can't exactly call in sick during dinner service. But it's wearing me down, to be honest.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Same as before — just gritting my teeth and getting through the shift. Not much else I can do when there's a full house waiting on their meals.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I already told you — I'm just getting through it. Not much else to say. Can we focus on what's causing this?
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I've already answered that. Twice. I'm coping, I'm working, it hurts. Can we move on and figure out what's wrong with my stomach?
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I'm not coping well, okay? I just keep repeating myself. Can we talk about the stomach issue now? That's why I'm here.
